papers.title: Highlights From the 2025 Pediatric Oncology East and Mediterranean Biennial Conference: Advancing Pediatric Oncology Across the Eastern Mediterranean.
papers.abstract: s, posters, expert sessions, and focused workshops.

papers.title: Therapeutic antibody delivery: vector tools to boost efficacy and affordability.
papers.abstract: Antibody (Ab)-based therapeutics have become powerful tools across diverse disease areas, with advances in bioengineering giving rise to next-generation molecules designed to outperform conventional Abs. Yet, large-scale production and purification of such complex proteins remain costly and can restrict patient access. A promising alternative is to improve in vivo expression capabilities, which will reduce manufacturing burdens and improve safety and tolerability. Multiple gene delivery platforms - ranging from mRNA and viral vectors to engineered cell therapies - have matured considerably, as a direct result of years of clinical experience and growing regulatory confidence. The rapid deployment of mRNA vaccines against SARS-CoV-2, the clinical success of adeno-associated virus (AAV)- and lentiviral-based interventions, and the approval of chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-T cell therapies highlight the potential of these technologies to transform how we deliver Ab therapeutics. While these approaches hold the promise to treat genetic aberrations in patients, they may also contribute considerably to advancing conventional Ab therapeutics against viral infections and other diseases through local persistence of the proteins. Looking forward, in situ expression may confer even more benefits for engineered Ab-like molecules, thereby compensating for possibly shorter half-lives and overcoming challenges in in vitro production and purification. Therefore, in this review, we critically evaluate how these established and emerging gene therapy platforms can be harnessed to expand access, and discuss possibilities to improve in situ availability through the choice of transient or stable expression systems to increase the efficacy of Abs and other therapeutic proteins. Furthermore, we explore the current landscape of technological advancements, identify key translational challenges, and project future directions for optimizing these approaches towards widely applicable clinical interventions.

papers.title: Distinct effector functions and synergy of CAR mRNA-engineered T cells and macrophages in the clearance of CD19(+) leukemia cells.
papers.abstract: Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) technology represents a groundbreaking advancement in gene therapy. While CAR-T cells are approved for specific types of leukemia, the therapeutic potential of CAR-macrophages remains largely unexplored. Additionally, potentially synergistic effects between CAR-T cells and CAR-macrophages in cancer cell clearance are still elusive. This study aimed to investigate the efficiency and mechanisms of human CAR-T cells and CAR-macrophages for clearing cancer cells using co-culture experiments. We encapsulated a second generation CD19 CAR mRNA or enhanced green fluorescent protein (Egfp) encoding control mRNA into lipid nanoparticles (LNP) using different methods. We transfected human primary T cells or macrophages with these LNP and studied their CAR expression using flow cytometry or confocal microscopy. Killing and phagocytosis assays were conducted to evaluate the impact of CAR-T cells and CAR-macrophages on CD19+ NALM6 leukemia and CD19- control cells. Motility analyses of macrophages, T cells, and cancer cells were done using live-cell imaging. Activated T cells upregulated the low-density lipoprotein receptor (LDLR), resulting in higher CAR surface expression post-transfection than in non-activated T cells. CAR-T cells efficiently induced death of lymphoma cells. CAR-macrophages cleared cancer cells mainly via phagocytosis and CAR mRNA altered the expression of both M1 and M2-associated genes. Interestingly, CAR macrophages also upregulated the Signal regulatory protein alpha (SIRPA), a receptor of the CD47 ligand expressed by cancer cells, and knockdown of SIRPA by siRNA efficiently counteracted this phenomenon. We noted a decrease in the motility of macrophages, T cells, and cancer cells in co-cultures containing CAR-transfected effector cells. Co-culturing CAR-T cells with CAR-macrophages synergistically enhanced tumor cell eradication. Our findings underscore the superior cancer cell-killing capability of CAR-T cells. Additionally, CAR-macrophages play a crucial role in clearing cancer cells through phagocytosis, thereby synergistically supporting the activity of CAR-T cells.

papers.title: Clinical trial results provide the rationale to protect dual HIV-specific T cells with a signaling-defective HIV fusion inhibitor.
papers.journal: Molecular therapy : the journal of the American Society of Gene Therapy
papers.abstract: Coupling the HIV fusion inhibitor C34 with CXCR4 (C34-CXCR4) protected CD4 T cells from all strains of HIV. Escape from C34-CXCR4 expression proved difficult, requiring more than 21 serial passages, mutations at conserved gp41 sites, and a complete loss of Vpu activity. A dose-escalation phase 1 clinical trial was performed in which up to 10 billion C34-CXCR4-expressing T cells were infused into people with HIV (PWH). While these infusions were safe, we observed limited persistence of these C34-CXCR4 T cells, lack of reconstitution of HIV-specific CD8 T cell responses, and no effect on time to viral rebound after an analytical treatment interruption. We hypothesized that inappropriate expression of a signaling-competent chemokine receptor resulted in the limited persistence of these engineered T cells, so we screened several C34-CXCR4 mutants to identify those that could not respond to CXCL12 and still could mediate potent antiviral activity. A single mutation (D97N) in CXCR4 fulfilled both criteria. T cells co-expressing dual HIV-specific chimeric antigen receptors (CARs) and C34-CXCR4 D97N maintained high C34 expression in vivo and controlled HIV replication better than unprotected dual CAR T cells, suggesting that this C34-CXCR4 D97N construct should be considered for future clinical development.

papers.title: From Vision to Viability: Developing Infrastructure for Cell and Gene Therapy Programs.
papers.abstract: Cell and gene therapies (CGTs), including chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy, tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes, and gene therapies, represent a transformative advancement in oncology and other disease states. Their delivery is complex, requiring multidisciplinary coordination, specialized infrastructure, and adherence to evolving regulatory standards. Institutions lacking robust frameworks risk inefficiencies, safety events, and non-compliance. Despite growing adoption, there is limited literature detailing operational strategies for implementing CGT programs. To provide a comprehensive, practice-oriented framework for developing institutional infrastructure that supports safe, efficient, and sustainable delivery of CGTs. This manuscript aims to outline actionable strategies for health systems to operationalize CGT programs across clinical, operational, and financial domains. This work is a descriptive analysis and synthesis of best practices derived from early adopters, accreditation standards, and institutional experience. It presents a structured roadmap encompassing strategic planning, governance, operational readiness, pharmacy integration, financial modeling, technology infrastructure, quality management, and outreach strategies. No statistical analysis was performed, as this is a conceptual and operational framework rather than an empirical study. The proposed framework emphasizes eight interdependent domains that collectively enable successful CGT program implementation. Strategic planning establishes vision alignment, readiness assessments, and governance structures to guide resource allocation and sustainability. Operational readiness focuses on developing standard operating procedures, multidisciplinary education, and escalation pathways to ensure seamless patient care. Product access requires early engagement with manufacturers and qualification processes for both commercial and investigational therapies. Pharmacy plays a pivotal role in protocol development, toxicity management, and biosafety handling, while financial and payer strategies address reimbursement complexities and long-term sustainability. Technology and data integration within electronic health records and dashboards support real-time coordination and compliance reporting. Quality management ensures adherence to accreditation standards and continuous improvement. Finally, outreach and referral strategies streamline patient access and foster collaboration with community providers. Together, these domains form a comprehensive blueprint for mitigating risk, optimizing patient safety, and ensuring timely access to therapy. CGTs offer unprecedented therapeutic potential but require deliberate infrastructure development to translate innovation into clinical practice. Institutions that invest in coordinated multidimensional readiness, spanning strategic, operational, and financial domains, will be best positioned to deliver high-quality, equitable care. This framework serves as a blueprint for centers seeking to implement CGT programs and adapt to the rapidly evolving therapeutic landscape.

papers.title: Human Genome Safe Harbor Sites: A Comprehensive Review of Criteria, Discovery, Features, and Applications.
papers.abstract: The stable and safe integration of exogenous DNA into the genome is crucial to both genetic engineering and gene therapy. Traditional transgenesis approaches, such as those using retroviral vectors, result in random genomic integration, posing the risk of insertional mutagenesis and transcriptional dysregulation. Safe harbor sites (SHSs), genomic loci that support reliable transgene expression without compromising endogenous gene function, genomic integrity, or cellular physiology, have been identified and characterized across various model organisms. Well-established SHSs such as AAVS1, ROSA26, and CLYBL are routinely utilized for targeted transgene integration in human cells. Recent advances in genome architecture, gene regulation, and genome editing technologies are driving the discovery of novel SHSs for precise and safe genetic modification. This review aims to provide a comprehensive overview of SHSs and their applications that will guide investigators in the choice of SHS, especially when complementary sites are needed for more than one transgene integration. First, it outlines safety and functional criteria that qualify a genomic site as a safe harbor site. It then discusses the two primary strategies for identifying SHSs: i) traditional lentiviral-based random transgenesis, and ii) modern genome-wide in silico screening followed by CRISPR-based validation. This review also provides an updated catalogue of currently known SHSs in the human genome, detailing their characteristics, uses, and limitations. Additionally, it discusses the diverse applications of SHSs in basic research, gene therapy, CAR T cell-based therapy, and biotechnological production systems. Finally, it concludes by highlighting challenges in identifying universally applicable SHSs and outlines future directions for their refinement and validation across biological systems.

papers.title: Rising Star Engineering the Genome for Curative Futures.
papers.abstract: As a professor of biomedicine in the School of Life Sciences at East China Normal University (ECNU), I am dedicated to developing advanced genome editing technologies for disease modeling and precise gene therapy. My foundational training at Hunan Normal University and Texas A&M University cultivated a deep interest in using engineered cellular and animal models to understand human diseases. Since 2013, my laboratory at ECNU has pioneered the use of TALEN and CRISPR/Cas9 for the rapid generation of knockout mouse and rat models for disease studies. Once stepped in genome editing field, I shifted my focus to advancing editing tools and developing gene therapy strategies for genetic disorders and cancer. My team has developed a suite of high-performance base editors for nuclear DNA, mitochondrial DNA, and RNA, broadening editing capabilities while enhancing precision and safety. Leveraging these technologies, we have designed several therapeutic strategies that have shown efficacy in cellular and animal models of genetic diseases. Through collaborative efforts, we have successfully translated genome editing into clinical applications, contributing to the treatment of patients with β-thalassemia. Additionally, we have developed a non-viral, site-specific CAR-T platform for lymphoma therapy. Looking forward, I aim to develop the next generation of long-fragment, site-specific integration technologies and accelerate clinical translation to bring transformative cures to more patients.

papers.title: Rapidly dissolving biomaterials for high-efficiency viral transduction.
papers.abstract: Cell and gene therapy represent the frontier of genetic medicine for treating devastating diseases, yet they continue to face a critical bottleneck: inefficient genetic cell modification. While viral vectors remain our most powerful genetic delivery tools, their application suffers from significant transport insufficiencies with most viral particles wasted before reaching their cell targets. This fundamental challenge undermines the efficiency, safety, and economic viability of these potentially transformative therapies. Current transduction enhancers provide only partial solutions with significant drawbacks. RetroNectin works only with retroviruses and hematopoietic cells, spinoculation is time consuming and laborious, while polycationic polymers like polybrene pose toxicity concerns that preclude clinical applications. In this report, we introduce DUCTS (Dissolving Ultrafast Cell Transduction Sponges), a transduction enhancer based on uncrosslinked alginate cryogels that works within five minutes and completely dissolves, enabling rapid and easy workflows. DUCTS demonstrates broad versatility, boosting cell transduction across a variety of viral vectors (gamma retrovirus, lentivirus, and adeno-associated virus) in both suspension and adherent cells. Notably, DUCTS achieves comparable transduction efficiency while reducing viral concentration requirements by an order of magnitude compared to standard protocols. This cell- and virus-agnostic platform streamlines gene transfer procedures, reduces viral consumption and associated costs, and enhances the accessibility and scalability of cell and gene therapies. STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE: In this report, we present DUCTS (Dissolving Ultrafast Cell Transduction Sponges), a major advance to improve transduction technology, addressing critical bottlenecks in cell therapy manufacturing. Unlike existing methods that require lengthy procedures, specialized equipment, or toxic additives, DUCTS achieves comparable transduction efficiency in just five minutes using a simple, dissolving alginate sponge. This platform works across multiple virus types (lentivirus, retrovirus, AAV) and cell types, dramatically reducing viral vector requirements by 10-fold while maintaining cell viability. A modified DUCTs can also simultaneously activate and transduce T cells in a single step to revolutionize CAR-T cell manufacturing. With exceptional shelf stability and GMP-compatible materials, DUCTS offers a practical solution to reduce costs, accelerate production timelines, and enhance accessibility of life-saving cell therapies for patients worldwide.

papers.title: Targeting immune heterogeneity in liver metastases through in vivo genetic engineering.
papers.abstract: The liver is both a central metabolic organ and a uniquely tolerogenic immune niche, which makes it a frequent site of liver metastases (LMS) and a challenging environment for immunotherapy. It is increasingly appreciated that resident liver cells- Kupffer cells, Ito cells, liver sinusoidal endothelial cells - as well as infiltrating myeloid and lymphoid populations, collectively establish an immunosuppressive microenvironment that favors metastatic seeding and progression. Advances in our understanding of these cell-cell interactions and the exclusion of effector T cells, have highlighted new therapeutic entry points. In parallel, innovations in gene-delivery platforms, from lentiviral and AAV vectors to lipid nanoparticles, and in engineered immune cells such as CAR-T and CAR-NK cells, are opening avenues to modulate the hepatic tumor niche. Here we review how these insights into liver immunobiology inform the design of next-generation immunotherapies for LMS, emphasizing opportunities and challenges in translating them to the clinic.

papers.title: Securing raw materials, reagents, and consumable supplies in the academic bioproduction UNITC network: because the chain is only as strong as its weakest link.
papers.abstract: Academic autologous cell manufacturing offers key advantages, including cost-effectiveness, accessibility, and flexibility. However, the management of Raw Materials, Reagents, and Consumables (RMRCs) is essential for ensuring product purity, safety, and effectiveness. Variations in RMRC quality can increase production costs and result in batch failures. This work from the GMP-Bioproduction group of the French Consortium in Advancing Cancer Cell and Gene Therapy (UNITC) outlines a multicenter study conducted from 2022 to 2024 across all 11 French academic cell and gene therapy facilities producing Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products, evaluating current RMRC management practices. The study highlights significant challenges, including supply shortages, reference changes, and inconsistent quality controls. While RMRC-related non-conformities accounted for only 6.8% of total issues, they frequently required complex procedural adjustments, resulting in added financial and operational burdens. Despite differences in production scale and ATMP types, all centers consistently evaluated the criticality of RMRC, reflecting strong alignment in risk assessment practices. To address these issues, the study proposes recommendations, including a unified RMRC risk classification system, harmonized quality assurance processes. These actions aim to strengthen regulatory compliance, enhance collaboration across academic centers, and improve the overall resilience of academic decentralized CAR-T cells manufacturing in France.

papers.title: Position statement from the British Society of Blood and Marrow Transplantation and Cellular Therapy on insertional oncogenesis in gene-engineered advanced cell therapy products for treatment of haematological disorders.
An Indigenous Workflow for Generating CD19 CAR-T Cells.
papers.abstract: FDA-approved CD19 CAR T cell therapy for treating B-cell lineage malignancies gave impetus to the adoptive immune/cell and gene therapy field. Although tested to a considerable extent, side effects such as immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS), immune effector cell-associated hematotoxicity (ICAHTs), the high cost of the therapy, and often complicated logistics make the accessibility of this CAR therapy far from the reach of many eligible patients. Therefore, the development of a safe, cost-effective, streamlined indigenous workflow for T-cell expansion, transduction, and CAR T-cell characterization is essential in a preclinical as well as clinical setting. We have optimized a method to develop CD19 CAR T cells from peripheral blood T cells from healthy donors. In this chapter, we describe an indigenous protocol for the generation and characterization of CD19 CAR T cells via lentiviral transduction to understand the immunology or possible downstream applications.

papers.title: Gene Therapy: Transforming the Battle Against Pancreatic Cancer.
papers.abstract: Pancreatic cancer remains one of the most aggressive and lethal malignancies, with a dismal prognosis despite advancements in conventional treatment modalities. Gene therapy has emerged as a promising approach to combat pancreatic cancer by targeting the underlying genetic alterations and harnessing the power of the immune system. This review explores the current landscape of gene therapy strategies for pancreatic cancer, including gene replacement therapy, gene silencing, immunotherapy enhancement, and oncolytic virotherapy. Gene replacement therapy aims to restore the function of tumor suppressor genes, such as TP53, while gene silencing targets oncogenes like KRAS (Kirsten rat sarcoma viral oncogene homolog) to inhibit tumor growth. Immunotherapy enhancement, particularly through chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy, has shown potential in overcoming the immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment. Oncolytic viruses, engineered to replicate in and destroy cancer cells selectively, have demonstrated efficacy in preclinical models and are being evaluated in clinical trials. Recent advances, including the successful treatment of a patient with advanced pancreatic cancer using neoantigen T-cell receptor gene therapy, highlight the potential of personalized gene therapy approaches. However, challenges such as precise gene delivery, tumor heterogeneity, and ethical considerations must be addressed to realize the potential of gene therapy for pancreatic cancer fully. Ongoing research and clinical trials are expected to facilitate the way for the development of safe and effective gene therapies, offering hope for improved outcomes in pancreatic cancer.

entities.paper_title: Development of RNase P ribozyme-based therapy against oral herpesvirus infections by HSV-1 and KSHV.
key_findings: SLC6A1-related disorder is defined by a specific functional loss of GABA transport, enabling a focused translational pipeline. Adeno-associated virus serotype 9-mediated gene replacement normalized EEG abnormalities in preclinical models. 4-phenylbutyrate, a pharmacological chaperone, restored GAT-1 trafficking and demonstrated seizure reduction in 80% of treated patients.

safety_data.summary: Behavioral adverse effects of levetiracetam should be monitored.

entities.paper_title: Emerging frontiers in genome editing: From CRISPR to next-generation technologies.
key_findings: SLC6A1-related disorder is defined by a specific functional loss of GABA transport, enabling a focused translational pipeline. Adeno-associated virus serotype 9-mediated gene replacement normalized EEG abnormalities in preclinical models. 4-phenylbutyrate, a pharmacological chaperone, has demonstrated seizure reduction in 80% of treated patients.

safety_data.summary: Behavioral adverse effects should be monitored for levetiracetam use.

entities.paper_title: Insect-Cell-Produced AAV2 Vectors Activate Myeloid Innate Immune Response Featuring NETosis.
key_findings: Insect-Cell-Produced AAV2 Vectors Activate Myeloid Innate Immune Response Featuring NETosis.

safety_data.summary: Insect-Cell-Produced AAV2 Vectors Activate Myeloid Innate Immune Response Featuring NETosis.

entities.paper_title: FDA Approval of the Congenital OTOF Gene Therapy Otarmeni Reshapes the Future of Hearing Care.
key_findings: The FDA has approved the OTOF gene therapy Otarmeni, marking a significant advancement in hearing care.
This approval highlights the potential of gene therapy to treat genetic disorders, with emerging therapies like gene therapy targeting LAMP2 being considered for Danon disease.

entities.paper_title: In vivo gene therapy with CAR-T cells.
key_findings: In vivo CAR gene delivery has demonstrated feasibility and therapeutic potential in recent clinical reports, offering an alternative to the logistically complex ex vivo manufacturing of CAR-T cells.

entities.paper_title: Dual-Gene CRISPR Editing via Peptide Dendrimers Regulates Redox Balance for Diabetic Wound Repair.
key_findings: A CRISPR/Cas9-based gene therapy was developed to simultaneously knockdown Keap1 and PHD2, activating the Nrf2 and HIF-1α pathways to combat oxidative stress in diabetic wounds.
Delivery was achieved using multifunctional peptide-modified lysine dendrimers (MsRNPs) that showed excellent biocompatibility and efficient gene editing in vitro.
A single topical application of the polyplexes in a diabetic mouse model robustly accelerated wound closure, enhanced collagen deposition, and promoted angiogenesis.

entities.paper_title: Emergent RNA targets in cardiovascular disease: likely candidates for treatment development.
key_findings: Noncoding RNAs (ncRNAs) are promising biomarkers and therapeutic targets for cardiovascular diseases due to their critical roles in molecular mechanisms and consistent dysregulation in these conditions.
RNA-based technologies have shown potent efficacy in preclinical studies for targeting cardiovascular disease pathways.
Ongoing clinical trials are evaluating RNA-based drugs, but challenges in delivery, specificity, toxicity, and cardiac uptake must be overcome for widespread clinical application.

entities.paper_title: Broadening activity of checkpoint blockade agents by intratumoral nucleoside cleavage.
key_findings: Intratumoral expression of the E. coli PNP gene, combined with the prodrug fludarabine, enhanced the efficacy of immune checkpoint inhibitors in a murine model of triple-negative breast cancer.

This gene therapy approach led to tumor cytoreduction, which was associated with favorable changes in the tumor microenvironment, including decreased PD1+ Tregs and increased granzyme B+ NK cells and MKI67+ T8 cells.
The strategy also demonstrated a systemic effect, suppressing the growth of anatomically distant tumors that did not express the PNP transgene.

entities.paper_title: Reprogramming the immune suppressive tumor microenvironment in glioma enhances the efficacy of immune-mediated gene therapy.
key_findings: Mutant IDH1 glioma cells exhibit reduced CD73 expression due to DNA hypermethylation, leading to lower adenosine levels.
CD73 inhibition alone did not improve survival in wtIDH1 glioma-bearing mice.
Combining CD73 blockade with immune-stimulatory Ad-TK/Ad-Flt3L gene therapy significantly enhanced therapeutic efficacy and increased anti-glioma effector T cell activity.

entities.paper_title: Engineering precision oncology: Targeting tumors and immune cells with lentiviral vectors.
key_findings: Lentiviral vectors are being engineered for precise targeting in cancer therapy to overcome limitations of broad tropism and serum sensitivity. Advances include pseudotyping with natural viral envelopes and next-generation retargeting strategies to enhance specificity for immune and tumor cells. Engineered virus-like particles (VLPs) are emerging as precision tools for in vivo delivery of gene editing complexes like CRISPR-Cas9, base-editing, and prime-editing.

entities.paper_title: Nanomedicines for DNA and interference RNA co-delivery: Combined gene therapy for Fabry disease.
key_findings: Golden SLN-based nanomedicines were developed for the co-delivery of GLA plasmid DNA and siRNA against Gb3 synthase to simultaneously promote α-Gal A production and inhibit Gb3 synthesis.
The nanomedicines increased α-Gal A activity and reduced Gb3 synthase expression and Gb3 levels for at least 15 days in an endothelial cell model of Fabry disease.
This study presents the first co-delivery nanomedicine enabling simultaneous gene supplementation and genetic substrate reduction therapy.

efficacy_data.metric: increased α-Gal A activity and reduced Gb3 synthase expression and Gb3 levels for at least 15 days

safety_data.summary: The nanomedicines were evaluated for hemocompatibility, and lipid composition and polysaccharide coating were determined to influence safety.

entities.paper_title: How I Treat: Chronic granulomatous disease.
key_findings: Preventative antimicrobial therapy is the backbone of management for Chronic granulomatous disease (CGD). Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation is increasingly favored as definitive therapy, while gene therapy remains a promising but investigational treatment.

entities.paper_title: Using an RNA binding protein to detect mRNA on lipid nanoparticles.
key_findings: A viral RNA binding protein, monoclonal antibody, and magnetic bead arrangement were used to develop an assay for detecting mRNA encapsulation efficiency in lipid nanoparticles (LNPs).
This method allows for the determination of whether mRNA is on the surface or internalized within the LNP.
The approach is suggested to be useful for optimizing the delivery of nucleic acids by LNPs for vaccines and gene therapy.

entities.paper_title: Achieving Maximum Efficiency of A549 Cell Transfection with a Plasmid Delivered by Polyethylenimine-Based Polyplexes.
key_findings: Increasing the PEI nitrogen to DNA phosphate ratio and/or increasing the polyplex concentration significantly improved transfection efficiency in A549 cells, from a few percent up to 100 percent.

entities.paper_title: AdamiForge: a modular reverse genetics tool for human adenovirus type 12 (HAdV-A12).
key_findings: A modular reverse genetics tool named AdamiForge was developed for HAdV-A12, allowing for the engineering of recombinant viruses.
Two recombinant viruses were created expressing modified versions of the viral DNA-binding protein (DBP) fused to eGFP or a FLAG-tag.
These modifications enabled the successful detection of the HAdV-A12 DBP by Western blotting and immunofluorescence, compensating for the lack of specific antibodies.

entities.paper_title: Dysregulation of glycogen metabolism in cardiomyopathy caused by glycogenin-1 (GYG1) missense variants.
key_findings: In GSD15, storage of abnormal glycogen in cardiomyocytes is associated with enrichment of specific proteins involved in glycogen metabolism, contributing to dysregulation of glycogen turnover.
This dysregulation results in polyglucosan accumulation, disruption of sarcomeric and mitochondrial architecture, and progressive fibrosis.
No disease-modifying therapy currently exists for GSD15; future strategies based on substrate reduction, enhancement of autophagy, or gene therapy require further investigation.

entities.paper_title: Defining the Role of Nurses in Gene Therapy for Haemophilia.
key_findings: Nurses play an essential role in the gene therapy pathway for haemophilia, including education, care coordination, bleed management, and long-term follow-up.
Their responsibilities span from eligibility assessment and patient education to peri-infusion care, adverse event detection, and psychosocial support.
Gene therapy for haemophilia requires well-trained nurses to ensure safe delivery, effective follow-up, and high-quality patient support.

entities.paper_title: Functional characterization of Furin-mediated lipoprotein lipase cleavage.
key_findings: Furin-mediated cleavage inactivates LPL in vivo.
Engineered furin-resistant LPL lowers plasma triglyceride levels more effectively than wild-type LPL.
Furin-resistant LPL does not cause adverse effects like hepatic steatosis.

safety_data.summary: The furin-resistant LPL did not cause adverse effects such as hepatic steatosis.

entities.paper_title: Reversible synaptic deficits in early-stage batten disease.
key_findings: Loss of CLN3 causes defective synaptic vesicle release and reduced synaptic strength, reflecting impairments in both pre- and postsynaptic function.
AAV9-mediated gene rescue at early disease stages corrected preexisting synaptic defects and restored function.
Early synaptic deficits provide sensitive and robust functional readouts for preclinical research.

entities.paper_title: Dual-Functional Chitosan-Hyaluronic Acid Dialdehyde Nanoparticles for CD44-Targeted Bcl-2 siRNA Delivery and Photothermal Therapy in Bladder Cancer.
key_findings: CS-HAD NPs enable CD44-targeted delivery of Bcl-2 siRNA and NIR-induced photothermal therapy, demonstrating a synergistic anti-cancer effect in vitro.
The nanoparticles effectively downregulated Bcl-2 expression at both protein and mRNA levels and showed good blood compatibility.

efficacy_data.metric: significant reductions in cell viability and Relative Growth Rate (RGR%)

safety_data.summary: Hemocompatibility markers remained within normal physiological ranges, indicating good blood compatibility.

entities.paper_title: Establishment of an immortalized erythroid model for Hb Bart's hydrops fetalis with homozygous α(0)-thalassemia Southeast Asian deletion.
key_findings: Chery-Bart's is an immortalized erythroid progenitor cell line derived from a fetus with homozygous α(0)-thalassemia Southeast Asian deletion (--SEA/--SEA).

The cell line exhibits sustained proliferation, stable recovery after cryopreservation, and retains key features of the parental cells, including the capacity for erythroid maturation and robust production of Hb Bart's (γ4).
This model provides a scalable resource for mechanistic studies and preclinical evaluation of therapeutic approaches for BHFS and other severe α-thalassemia syndromes.

entities.paper_title: Quantification of a serum titin fragment reflects dystrophin restoration in mdx mice and disease severity in patients with dystrophinopathies.
key_findings: A 100 kDa serum titin fragment was identified as a biomarker for dystrophinopathies.
The concentration of this fragment negatively correlated with dystrophin expression in mdx mice treated with AAV9-CK8-μDys5 gene therapy.
This fragment shows a steeper age-related decline in DMD patients compared to BMD patients, suggesting its potential as a pharmacodynamic biomarker.

efficacy_data.metric: -0.76 Pearson correlation between serum titin concentration and (micro)dystrophin expression in mdx mice

safety_data.summary: No safety data mentioned in the provided abstract.

entities.paper_title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus modulates gut microbiota and tyrosine metabolism to potentiate PD-1-mediated anti-tumor immunity in triple-negative breast cancer.
key_findings: Intratumoral PD-1 gene therapy inhibited tumor growth and promoted anti-tumor immunity but induced intestinal side effects.
The probiotic Lactobacillus rhamnosus enhanced the anti-tumor efficacy of PD-1 gene therapy and alleviated intestinal injury.
L. rhamnosus achieved this by modulating gut microbiota composition, reducing inflammation, and altering tyrosine metabolism.

safety_data.summary: Intratumoral PD-1 gene therapy induced intestinal injury, which was alleviated by co-treatment with Lactobacillus rhamnosus.

entities.paper_title: Diagnostic and therapeutic applications of the glycan biomarker H3N2b in GM1 Gangliosidosis.
key_findings: The oligosaccharide H3N2b was identified as a highly specific and sensitive biomarker for diagnosing GM1 gangliosidosis across plasma, urine, and CSF.
H3N2b levels correlated with disease severity and decreased following AAV9-mediated GLB1 gene therapy, closely paralleling changes in β-galactosidase activity.
This demonstrates H3N2b's utility as a pharmacodynamic biomarker for monitoring therapeutic response.

efficacy_data.metric: decrease in H3N2b concentrations following treatment

safety_data.summary: Not mentioned in the provided abstract.

entities.paper_title: Practical guidelines for producing non-replicating canine adenovirus vectors.
key_findings: This paper provides a detailed protocol for producing non-replicative canine adenovirus type 2 (CAV-2) vectors. The process involves expressing and expanding the vector in a complementing AD-293 cell line, followed by purification via ion-exchange liquid chromatography and titration using the Improved Kärber method.

entities.paper_title: Adenoviral Vectors in Gene Therapy: A Detailed Overview.
key_findings: Adenoviral vectors are a highly researched gene therapy platform with high transduction efficiency, large transgene capacity, and broad tropism, but their clinical use is limited by immunogenicity, hepatotoxicity, scalability issues, and lack of durable expression.
Recent vector engineering innovations, such as rare serotypes, capsid modifications, and third-generation vectors, aim to overcome these challenges.
AdVs are being integrated with other technologies like CRISPR-Cas9, nanotechnology, and AI to enhance their precision and adaptability in treating genetic disorders and cancer.

safety_data.summary: Persistent hurdles include vector immunogenicity and hepatotoxicity.

entities.paper_title: Optimization of Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Expression from Adeno-Associated Virus Vectors.
key_findings: Promoter strength and cassette architecture are dominant determinants of AAV-AAT potency.
Full-length, single-stranded vectors with robust regulatory elements provide the highest expression.
Codon optimization and modifications to polyadenylation signals or enhancer combinations did not improve expression.

safety_data.summary: The goal is to reduce the required vector dose to levels below those associated with severe adverse events.

entities.paper_title: Precision medicine in paediatrics: Progress and priorities.
key_findings: Precision medicine in pediatrics, including pharmacogenomics, pharmacometrics, and advanced therapies like gene and nucleic acid treatments, can personalize care and optimize treatment.
These innovations can reduce trial-and-error prescribing, accelerate safe and effective treatment identification, and reduce drug toxicity.
Implementation faces challenges related to cost, workforce preparedness, governance, and equity.

entities.paper_title: Research Hotspots and Emerging Trends in Osteoporosis Epigenetics.
key_findings: Epigenetic mechanisms are a core regulatory factor in osteoporosis, linking genetic predisposition and environmental influences.
Future research will focus on miRNAs in OP epigenetics and identifying therapeutic targets.
Emerging therapeutic strategies include gene therapy, stem cell-based interventions, and small-molecule targeted compounds.

entities.paper_title: Gene therapy rescues cone function in opn1mw (-/-) /opn1sw (-/-) /Nrl (-/-) mice, an all-cone model of blue cone monochromacy.
key_findings: AAV-mediated human L-opsin gene therapy restored cone function, replenished phototransduction proteins, and partially regenerated cone outer segment membrane structure in an all-cone mouse model of BCM.
Treatment was effective in both younger (1 month) and older (5 months) mice, with rescue effects persisting for at least 5 months post-treatment.
These findings suggest that opsin-deficient cones remain viable targets for gene therapy, supporting its clinical potential for BCM patients.

entities.paper_title: Global age-related seroprevalence for adeno-associated virus serotype 9 immunoglobulin G.
key_findings: AAV9-Ab immunoglobulin G titers vary by age, with a higher likelihood of titers ≥1:100 in the 0-month age group compared to others.
Titers decline up to 12 months of age and then steadily increase from 12 to 24 months.
A greater proportion of individuals from Russia had AAV9-Ab titers ≥1:100 compared to other countries.

entities.paper_title: Development of a secretable frataxin for enhanced efficacy in treating Friedreich's Ataxia.
key_findings: Engineered Cross-Correction, using a secretable frataxin delivered via a single intra-CSF AAV injection, achieved broad protein repletion in the heart, dorsal root ganglia, and cerebellum in mouse and non-human primate models.
This approach rescued both cardiac and neurological phenotypes in FA mouse models, marking the first demonstration of dual correction with a single, minimally invasive administration.
The benefits were achieved without widespread transduction, reducing vector burden and associated toxicity.

safety_data.summary: Reduced vector burden and associated toxicity by avoiding widespread transduction.

entities.paper_title: Normothermic perfusion of human livers for profiling lentiviral vector pharmacokinetics and transduction.
key_findings: Normothermic machine perfusion (NMP) of human livers provides a physiologically relevant model to assess lentiviral vector (LV) delivery and transduction.
Rapid LV clearance was observed, with less than 1% remaining in perfusate after 10 minutes, and integrated viral copy numbers per cell reached 0.07-0.13 with detectable GFP expression.
This study supports NMP as a platform to enhance the translation of LV therapy into human clinical applications by reducing reliance on animal models.

entities.paper_title: MicroRNA-based targeting strategies enable AAV-mediated, tissue-selective gene expression in adipose tissue and skeletal muscle of mice.
key_findings: Liver-specific miR122 effectively suppressed AAV9-driven liver expression while preserving adipose tissue targeting efficiency.
AAVMYO, in combination with a muscle-specific promoter and target sites for miR208a, confined transgene expression to skeletal muscle while successfully de-targeting the liver and heart.
Both miRNA-based approaches outperformed conditionally self-silencing constructs that utilized artificial miRNAs.

entities.paper_title: RAAVioli: A comprehensive approach to characterizing AAV vector integrations and rearrangements.
key_findings: RAAVioli is a robust and versatile computational method for the comprehensive analysis of AAV integration sites and vector rearrangements across long- and short-read sequencing platforms.
Short-read sequencing provided superior throughput, uncovering more integration sites and enabling precise quantification of clonal abundance, which is essential for evaluating gene therapy safety.
Benchmarking analyses highlighted RAAVioli's higher sensitivity and accuracy relative to existing tools, establishing it as a reliable and scalable resource for AAV-based gene therapy studies.

safety_data.summary: The tool enables precise quantification of clonal abundance, an essential parameter for evaluating the safety of gene therapy protocols.

entities.paper_title: Intracisternal IGF-1 gene delivery attenuates early anxiety-like behavior but not dopaminergic neurodegeneration in a 6-OHDA rat model of parkinsonism.
key_findings: Intracisternal IGF-1 gene delivery attenuated early anxiety-like behavior in a 6-OHDA rat model of parkinsonism.
However, IGF-1 overexpression did not prevent the loss of dopaminergic neurons in the ventral tegmental area.
This demonstrates a dissociation between the behavioral effects of IGF-1 and its impact on dopaminergic neurodegeneration.

entities.paper_title: Pharmacotherapies for Gaucher disease: what's new on the horizon?
key_findings: Current treatments for Gaucher disease include enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) and substrate reduction therapy (SRT). New and emerging therapies include small molecule drugs, gene therapy, and nanotechnology. Future efforts should focus on brain-penetrant therapies for neuronopathic GD and GBA1-related Parkinson disease.

entities.paper_title: Genetically modified bone marrow cells halt mitral cell loss by modulating inflammation and protecting against DNA damage.
key_findings: Transplantation of bone marrow stem cells overexpressing IGF1 virtually blocked neuronal death in a PCD mouse model.
The therapy works by regulating neuroinflammation, increasing reactive microglia and reducing proinflammatory microglia.
IGF1 overexpression also dramatically reduced DNA damage via the IGFBP3 pathway, enhancing neuroprotection.

entities.paper_title: Redefining Parkinson's Care: The Promise of Nanotechnology and Artificial Intelligence.
key_findings: AI is emerging as a complementary tool for designing nanocarriers and predicting drug interactions, with liposomes and metallic nanoparticles being important platforms for dopamine replacement, gene therapy, and neuroinflammation modulation.

Integration of AI with biologically relevant PK/PD models and personalized nanomedicine strategies is suggested to overcome challenges like nanoparticle toxicity and variability in AI model performance.

This review summarizes current advances in nanomedicine and AI-driven approaches for PD, discussing their mechanisms, therapeutic targets, and future perspectives in achieving disease-modifying interventions.

safety_data.summary: Challenges include nanoparticle toxicity, long-term safety, and variability in AI model performance.

entities.paper_title: Machine-guided design for bioengineering gene therapy vectors: Where are we and what lies ahead?
key_findings: Computational approaches, including machine learning, are enabling the design of finely engineered viral vectors for gene therapy by exploring vast protein sequence spaces beyond traditional methods.
These computational methods support viral vector bioengineering by targeting stability, structural modeling, functional prediction, and generating novel variants.
The field is moving toward more integrated and holistic design strategies, with a transformative potential for the next generation of viral gene therapies.

entities.paper_title: Organoids: From Bench to Bedside Applications.
key_findings: Organoids provide physiologically relevant 3D models for biomedical research, addressing limitations of 2D cultures and reducing species-specific discrepancies.
Organoids are valuable for propagating hard-to-culture pathogens like human rhinovirus C and norovirus, and for studying viral pathogenesis of SARS-CoV-2 and Zika virus.
Organoids have translational utility for antiviral drug screening and preclinical assessment, and can be integrated with technologies like CRISPR gene editing and 3D bioprinting.

entities.paper_title: Decoding the role of chromatin context in the off-target effects of CRISPR gene editing with EGOLD.
key_findings: The EGOLD method was developed for high-throughput detection of CRISPR off-target effects and identification of chromatin context bias.
Off-target effects of CRISPR/Cas9 and base editors strongly correlate with open chromatin regions.
Incorporating chromatin context data into machine learning models significantly improves off-target prediction accuracy.

safety_data.summary: Off-target effects were found to occur at a significantly greater frequency in open chromatin regions than in closed chromatin regions.

entities.paper_title: Artificial Intelligence-Assisted CRISPR Gene Editing: Current Advances, Clinical Challenges, and Future Directions in Precision Medicine.
key_findings: AI-driven computational models significantly enhance prediction accuracy, algorithmic efficiency, and translational potential in CRISPR-Cas systems for genetic diseases.
Deep learning, machine learning, and reinforcement learning approaches are key to these improvements.
Ethical challenges, algorithmic bias, and data security remain critical barriers to clinical adoption.

safety_data.summary: Ethical challenges, algorithmic bias, and data security concerns are mentioned as critical barriers to clinical adoption.

entities.paper_title: Transcriptomic study of WSSV infection in Litopenaeus vannamei lymphoid organ via single nuclei RNA sequencing.
key_findings: A single nuclei RNA-seq study was performed on the lymphoid organ of Litopenaeus vannamei to understand the impact of WSSV infection.
The study constructed the first penaeid shrimp lymphoid organ cell atlas and identified marker genes for different cell types.
Multiple genes of interest were identified as potential targets for CRISPR gene editing to develop WSSV-resistant shrimp stocks.

entities.paper_title: Generation of Allogeneic CAR-T Circumvents Functional Deficits in Patient-Derived Autologous Product for Glioblastoma.
key_findings: Autologous CAR-T cells derived from glioblastoma patients show functional deficits and reduced efficacy compared to healthy donor cells in a pre-clinical model.
Allogeneic CAR-T cells, generated using CRISPR to knock out the TCR, demonstrate comparable pre-clinical efficacy to autologous models.
This work suggests that allogeneic approaches may be a more effective alternative to autologous CAR-T therapy for glioblastoma.

entities.paper_title: Precise, specific gene editing via a compact GoCas12m-FokI chimeric nuclease.
key_findings: The GoCas12m-FokI chimeric nuclease is a compact gene editor that achieves high-efficiency editing at clinically relevant targets with no detectable off-target activity.
Its small size facilitates delivery via adeno-associated virus and other cargo-limited vectors.
The system offers a powerful alternative for therapeutic genome editing and a promising tool for in vivo gene therapy applications.

safety_data.summary: No detectable off-target activity at in silico-predicted sites, as confirmed by targeted deep sequencing.
